description:
<title>Generate a daily repository-activity summary from a raw log</title>

  <goal>
    You are working as an artifact-repository curator.  
    A raw activity log located at  
    /home/user/repo-activity.log  
    records every upload or deletion that occurred in all binary repositories.  

    Your job is to:
      1. Parse that single log file and consider only the entries whose date is 2023-08-15.
      2. Create a text summary file called  
         /home/user/daily_summary_2023-08-15.txt
      3. Strictly follow the required output format shown below so that automated
         tests can verify your work.
  </goal>

  <output_format>
    The summary file must contain exactly five lines, terminated with UNIX line-feeds
    (LF, “\n”).  
    1. Line-1 – a plain header in the form (no extra spaces):  
       Summary for 2023-08-15
    2. Line-2 – repository “libs-release”, alphabetically the first repo name:  
       libs-release: UPLOADS=<count> DELETES=<count>
    3. Line-3 – repository “libs-snapshot”:  
       libs-snapshot: UPLOADS=<count> DELETES=<count>
    4. Line-4 – repository “plugins-release”:  
       plugins-release: UPLOADS=<count> DELETES=<count>
    5. Line-5 – a footer containing overall totals:  
       TOTAL UPLOADS: <total_uploads>, TOTAL DELETES: <total_deletes>

    • Repository lines MUST appear in case-sensitive, alphabetical order by the
      repository name exactly as above.  
    • Each key (UPLOADS / DELETES) and its integer count are joined by a single
      equals sign with no spaces.  
    • Counts must reflect only the chosen day (2023-08-15).  
    • No blank lines, no trailing spaces.
  </output_format>

  <verification_hint>
    When you believe the summary is correct, display its contents to the console so
    the evaluator can see it immediately.  The entire task can be completed with
    three or four typical shell commands (grep, awk, sort, etc.), but you may use
    any CLI utilities available in the container.
  </verification_hint>
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth data for automated testing
    DO NOT reveal any of this in the public task description.
  -->

  <preexisting_files>
    <!-- Raw log already present when the task starts -->
    <file path="/home/user/repo-activity.log"><![CDATA[
2023-08-14 21:34:11 libs-release com.example:foo:0.9.0 UPLOAD
2023-08-15 00:05:22 libs-snapshot com.example:bar:2.0.0-SNAPSHOT UPLOAD
2023-08-15 01:45:10 libs-release com.example:foo:1.0.0 UPLOAD
2023-08-15 02:10:05 libs-release com.example:foo:1.0.0 DELETE
2023-08-15 08:22:33 plugins-release com.company:plugin:3.1.4 UPLOAD
2023-08-15 09:10:00 plugins-release com.company:plugin:3.1.3 DELETE
2023-08-16 10:00:00 libs-snapshot com.example:bar:2.0.1-SNAPSHOT UPLOAD
]]></file>
  </preexisting_files>

  <expected_output_file path="/home/user/daily_summary_2023-08-15.txt"><![CDATA[
Summary for 2023-08-15
libs-release: UPLOADS=1 DELETES=1
libs-snapshot: UPLOADS=1 DELETES=0
plugins-release: UPLOADS=1 DELETES=1
TOTAL UPLOADS: 3, TOTAL DELETES: 2
]]></expected_output_file>

  <permissions>
    The /home/user directory and its contents are owned by user:user with u+rw
    permissions; no root privileges are required to create or modify the summary
    file inside it.
  </permissions>
